acceptance: Replay(sessionLogPath) reads JSONL and renders human-readable conversation; shows system prompt, each turn, tool calls with I/O, token counts, cost per turn; output suitable for terminal; unit test with fixture session log
description: Implement session replay renderer per FEAT-005.
title: Implement session replay

acceptance: forge -p 'prompt' runs agent and prints result; forge -p @file reads from file; stdin prompt works; --json flag outputs JSON; exit codes 0/1/2; config loaded from .forge/config.yaml with env and flag overrides; forge log lists sessions; forge replay renders session; functional tests with built binary
description: Implement the forge CLI binary per FEAT-006 and SD-002.
title: Implement standalone forge CLI

acceptance: OpenAIProvider implements forge.Provider; uses openai-go SDK; configurable base URL; sends tools as OpenAI function defs; parses tool calls from response; reports token usage; retries with backoff on 429/500/503; 5s connection timeout; unit tests with mock server pass
description: Implement the OpenAI-compatible provider covering LM Studio, Ollama, OpenAI, Azure, etc. per FEAT-003.
title: Implement OpenAI-compatible provider

acceptance: BashTool implements forge.Tool; executes via sh -c in working dir; captures stdout/stderr/exit code; kills on timeout; truncates output >1MB; stdin from /dev/null; unit tests pass
description: Implement the shell execution tool per FEAT-002 requirements.
title: Implement bash tool

acceptance: forge.go exports Request, Result, Status, Provider interface, Tool interface, Message, TokenUsage types; go vet passes; types match SD-001 domain model
description: Implement the public API types in the forge root package as defined in SD-001 domain model.
title: Define core types: Request, Result, Provider, Tool, Message

acceptance: Logger writes JSONL events (session.start, llm.request, llm.response, tool.call, session.end); events have session_id, seq, type, ts, data fields; implements forge.EventCallback; log dir configurable; graceful on unwritable dir; unit tests: write events then read back and verify
description: Implement JSONL session event logging per FEAT-005 and SD-001 log format.
title: Implement session logger and event types

acceptance: DefaultPricing table with common models; CostEstimate(model, usage) returns USD; unknown model returns -1; local models return 0; Result.CostUSD accumulated across loop iterations; unit tests for known/unknown/local models
description: Implement per-model pricing table and cost estimation per FEAT-005 and SD-001.
title: Implement pricing table and cost tracking

acceptance: EditTool implements forge.Tool; find-replace with uniqueness check; fails on not-found; fails on ambiguous match; rejects empty old_string; unit tests pass
description: Implement the find-replace edit tool per FEAT-002 requirements.
title: Implement edit tool

acceptance: ReadTool implements forge.Tool; reads files relative to working dir; supports offset/limit; errors on missing file; errors on binary content; unit tests pass
description: Implement the file read tool per FEAT-002 requirements.
title: Implement read tool

acceptance: forge.Run() orchestrates prompt->model->tools loop; terminates on text-only response (success); terminates on max iterations (iteration_limit); terminates on ctx cancel (cancelled); terminates on provider error after retries (error); accumulates tokens across iterations; calls EventCallback for every event; all exit conditions tested with mock provider
description: Implement the core agent loop in forge.Run() per FEAT-001 and SD-001.
title: Implement agent loop engine

acceptance: WriteTool implements forge.Tool; creates parent dirs; overwrites existing files; returns bytes written; unit tests pass
description: Implement the file write tool per FEAT-002 requirements.
title: Implement write tool

acceptance: go.mod exists with module path; go build ./... succeeds; .golangci.yml configured; Makefile with build/test/lint targets; .forge/ directory for session logs
description: Bootstrap the Go module, create the package directory structure from SD-001 architecture, configure golangci-lint per go-std concern, and add a Makefile.
